Package io.cloudstub.junit

Class CloudStubExtension

java.lang.Object
io.cloudstub.junit.CloudStubExtension
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.AfterAllCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.AfterTestExecutionCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.BeforeAllCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.BeforeTestExecutionCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.Extension
public final class CloudStubExtension extends Object implements org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.BeforeAllCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.AfterAllCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.BeforeTestExecutionCallback, org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.AfterTestExecutionCallback
JUnit extension (JUnit 5 and 6) that manages the CloudStub lifecycle around a test class and applies fault injection annotations (SimulateThrottle, SimulateTimeout, SimulateNetworkBrownout) to individual test methods.

Usage - zero-boilerplate (@ExtendWith)

 @ExtendWith(CloudStubExtension.class)
 class MyTest { ... }

Usage — port access (@RegisterExtension)

 @RegisterExtension
 static CloudStubExtension cloudMock = new CloudStubExtension()
         .withService(new CloudStubSqsService());

 @Test
 void myTest() {
     int port = cloudMock.port();
 }

Auto-downloaded modules (withModules)

 @RegisterExtension
 static CloudStubExtension cloudMock = new CloudStubExtension()
         .withModules("sqs", "secretsmanager");

The named module jars are fetched from Maven Central during beforeAll and registered, so the modules need not be on the test classpath. withService(io.cloudstub.core.spi.CloudStubService) registers an in-process instance (for stubs that are not published artifacts); withModule(java.lang.String)/withModules(java.lang.String...) downloads a published module by id. Downloads are cached under ~/.cloudstub/modules by default, so a module fetched once is reused across runs and JVMs. A module that ships a client-side AWS SDK component (currently only S3) cannot be provisioned by download and must be a test dependency; see the JUnit Extension reference for details.

Fault injection

 @SimulateThrottle(service = "sqs")
 @Test
 void throttledTest() { ... }

Fault state is always cleaned up after each test method, even if the test throws. Each test class gets an independent CloudStub instance — one class stopping never affects another class's running instance.

  • Constructor Details

    • CloudStubExtension

      public CloudStubExtension()

  • Method Details

    • withService

      public CloudStubExtension withService(CloudStubService service)
      Registers a service implementation to be installed when CloudStub starts. Use this for stubs that are not published artifacts (custom or test-local services). Must be called before the extension starts (i.e. before any test runs).
      Returns:
      this for fluent chaining

    • withServices

      public CloudStubExtension withServices(CloudStubService... services)
      Registers several service implementations at once; the plural of withService(CloudStubService). Must be called before the extension starts.
      Returns:
      this for fluent chaining

    • withModule

      public CloudStubExtension withModule(String serviceId)
      Records a published service module to auto-download by id. The module jar ( io.github.cloudstub:cloudstub-<serviceId>) is fetched from Maven Central during beforeAll if it is not already cached, then registered without needing the module on the test classpath. Repeated ids collapse to a single fetch. Must be called before the extension starts.
      Parameters:
      serviceId - service id (e.g. sqs)
      Returns:
      this for fluent chaining

    • withModules

      public CloudStubExtension withModules(String... serviceIds)
      Records several published service modules to auto-download by id; the plural of withModule(String). Must be called before the extension starts.
      Returns:
      this for fluent chaining

    • withModules

      public CloudStubExtension withModules(List<String> serviceIds)
      Records several published service modules to auto-download by id; the plural of withModule(String). Must be called before the extension starts.
      Returns:
      this for fluent chaining

    • withModuleVersion

      public CloudStubExtension withModuleVersion(String version)
      Sets the version of the modules requested via withModule(java.lang.String)/withModules(java.lang.String...). Defaults to CoreVersion.current().
      Returns:
      this for fluent chaining

    • withModulesCacheDir

      public CloudStubExtension withModulesCacheDir(Path cacheDir)
      Sets the directory used to cache downloaded module jars. Defaults to ~/.cloudstub/modules. A persistent directory lets a module fetched once be reused across test classes, runs, and JVMs.
      Returns:
      this for fluent chaining

    • withMavenBaseUrl

      public CloudStubExtension withMavenBaseUrl(String mavenBaseUrl)
      Sets the Maven repository base URL module jars are downloaded from. Defaults to Maven Central.
      Returns:
      this for fluent chaining

      public void beforeAll(@NonNull org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtensionContext context)
      Specified by:
      beforeAll in interface org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.BeforeAllCallback

    • afterAll

      public void afterAll(@NonNull org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtensionContext context)
      Specified by:
      afterAll in interface org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.AfterAllCallback

    • beforeTestExecution

      public void beforeTestExecution(org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtensionContext context)
      Specified by:
      beforeTestExecution in interface org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.BeforeTestExecutionCallback

    • afterTestExecution

      public void afterTestExecution(@NonNull org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtensionContext context)
      Specified by:
      afterTestExecution in interface org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.AfterTestExecutionCallback

    • port

      public int port()
      Returns the port the embedded server is listening on. Only valid inside a test method (after beforeAll and before afterAll).
